Title: Configurable product PDP renders correctly with configuration widget
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${FRONT_URL}/products-with-options/configurations/build-the-bike-of-your-dreams/bike-with-options
2. Wait for text "Configure" to appear (allow up to 10 seconds for Vue.js rendering)
3. Take accessibility snapshot of the full page
4. Locate the "Configure the parameters" widget section
5. Locate the accordion section with label "Select one"
6. Locate the subtitle text "Personalize your selection further (optional)"
7. Locate the radio button group and verify all 5 options are present: Rear wheel, Engine, Seat, Pedals, None
8. Verify the "None" radio button is selected by default
9. Locate the quantity stepper and verify it shows 0
10. Verify the "Add to cart" button is disabled when quantity is 0
11. Verify the "In stock" badge is visible showing available units
Expected Result:
- Page loads without errors
- Widget header reads exactly: "Configure the parameters"
- Accordion label reads exactly: "Select one Personalize your selection further (optional)"
- All 5 radio options are displayed: Rear wheel, Engine, Seat, Pedals, None
- "None" radio button is checked by default
- Quantity stepper shows 0 on page load
- "Add to cart" button is disabled at quantity 0
- "In stock" badge is visible

Title: Select each radio option and verify price updates
Steps:
1. Navigate to the Bike with options PDP and wait for "Configure" text
2. Record the base price shown ($350.00)
3. Click the radio button for "Rear wheel, 26", double-wall rim, motorized"
4. Record the updated total price displayed
5. Click the radio button for the Engine option (200CC 250CC 4-Stroke Engine...)
6. Record the updated total price displayed
7. Click the radio button for "Seat"
8. Record the updated total price displayed
9. Click the radio button for "Pedals"
10. Record the updated total price displayed
11. Click the radio button for "None"
12. Verify the total price returns to the base price
Expected Result:
- Selecting "Rear wheel" updates total price to $526.00 ($350 + $176 extended)
- Selecting "Engine" updates total price to $575.00 ($350 + $225)
- Selecting "Seat" updates total price to $365.00 ($350 + $15)
- Selecting "Pedals" updates total price to $364.00 ($350 + $14)
- Selecting "None" returns total to $350.00
- Each radio selection: selected option radio button is checked, previously selected option is unchecked, only one option can be selected at a time
- Accordion subtitle updates to show selected option name when an option is selected

Title: Price display per option row shows correct unit price, quantity, and extended price
Steps:
1. Navigate to the Bike with options PDP and wait for "Configure" text
2. Observe the option rows in the accordion section
3. For "Rear wheel, 26", double-wall rim, motorized": read the displayed unit price, quantity, and extended price
4. For the Engine option: read the displayed unit price, quantity, and extended price
5. For "Seat": read the displayed unit price, quantity, and extended price
6. For "Pedals": read the displayed unit price, quantity, and extended price
7. Verify extended price = unit price x quantity for each option
Expected Result:
- Rear wheel: unit price $88.00 (salePrice), qty 2, extended $176.00
- Engine: unit price $225.00, qty 1, extended $225.00
- Seat: unit price $15.00, qty 1, extended $15.00
- Pedals: unit price $14.00, qty 1, extended $14.00
- Extended price = unit price x quantity for all options

Title: Quantity stepper starts at 0 and Add to Cart is disabled until quantity is increased
Section: Storefront > Configurable Products > Quantity and Cart
Steps:
1. Navigate to the Bike with options PDP and wait for "Configure" text
2. Verify quantity stepper shows 0
3. Verify "Add to cart" button is disabled (greyed out or not clickable) at qty 0
4. Click the + (increment) button on the quantity stepper once
5. Verify quantity changes to 1
6. Verify "Add to cart" button becomes enabled/active
7. Click the + button again
8. Verify quantity changes to 2
9. Click the - (decrement) button
10. Verify quantity returns to 1 and "Add to cart" remains enabled
11. Manually type 0 in the quantity field (if editable)
12. Verify "Add to cart" becomes disabled again when quantity is 0
Expected Result:
- Quantity stepper initializes at 0 on page load
- "Add to cart" button is disabled when quantity = 0
- Clicking + increments quantity by 1
- At quantity = 1, "Add to cart" button becomes enabled
- Clicking + again increments to 2
- Clicking - decrements to 1; button remains enabled
- Setting quantity to 0 (via direct entry) disables "Add to cart" again

12. Verify the item price in the cart

- After selecting Seat: total price shows $365.00
- After setting qty to 1: "Add to cart" button is enabled
- Clicking "Add to cart": success notification appears (e.g. "Product added to cart" or similar)
- Cart icon updates to show 1 item
- Cart page shows: "Bike with options" (product name), configuration detail showing "Seat" option selected, price $365.00
- No errors in browser console

3. Record the current scroll position (scrollY)
4. Ensure "None" is selected (default)
5. Click the radio button for the Engine option (very long product name: 200CC 250CC 4-Stroke...)
6. Record the scroll position immediately after the click
7. Verify scroll delta = 0 (no auto-scroll occurred)
8. Click the radio button for "Seat" (short name)
9. Record the scroll position after this click

11. Click "Pedals" and verify scroll position remains unchanged
Expected Result:
Regression check for VCST-4612 fix (Sprint 26-03):
- Clicking any radio button does NOT change the page scroll position
- scrollY remains constant across all radio button transitions: None->Engine, Engine->Seat, Seat->Pedals
- Accordion subtitle updates to reflect the selected option name without causing scroll

Title: Price calculation is accurate for all option combinations
Steps:
1. Navigate to the Bike with options PDP and wait for "Configure" text
2. Record base product price (None selected): $350.00
3. Select "Seat" and record total price
4. Select "Pedals" and record total price
5. Select "Rear wheel" and record total price
6. Select "Engine" and record total price
7. Select "None" and record total price
8. Calculate expected totals: Seat=$350+$15=$365, Pedals=$350+$14=$364, Rear wheel=$350+$176=$526, Engine=$350+$225=$575, None=$350
9. Compare displayed totals with calculated totals for each option
Expected Result:
- None selected: total = $350.00 (base price only)
- Seat selected: total = $365.00 ($350 base + $15 option)
- Pedals selected: total = $364.00 ($350 base + $14 option)
- Rear wheel selected: total = $526.00 ($350 base + $176 extended = $88 x 2)
- Engine selected: total = $575.00 ($350 base + $225 extended = $225 x 1)
- All totals match the formula: Total = Base Price + (Option Unit Price x Option Quantity)

7. Verify extended price = unit price x quantity for each row
Expected Result:
- Rear wheel: unit $88.00 (salePrice), qty x2, extended $176.00
- Engine: unit $225.00, qty x1, extended $225.00
- Seat: unit $15.00, qty x1, extended $15.00
- Pedals: unit $14.00, qty x1, extended $14.00
- Extended = unit x qty for all options

Title: Admin: Create a new configurable product in the catalog

3. Navigate to the Products with Options category (or a suitable test category)

7. Enter SKU: "QA-CONFIG-TEST-001"
8. Set a price: $100.00
9. Click Save

Expected Result:

- New product form loads with product type selection
- "Configurable" product type is available as an option
- Product is created successfully with name "QA Test Configurable Product" and SKU "QA-CONFIG-TEST-001"
